title: gh#64: R2.4 — Add planRecordSuccess + planRecordFailure
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/64.
Backlog section: Candidate 2 — Post Lifecycle aggregate.

## Context

Fourth PR in Candidate 2. Migrates the success and failure paths of `publishPost` to the aggregate. After this PR, the worker's `publishPost` is a thin orchestrator over pure decisions.

## Scope

- Add to `packages/shared/src/post/aggregate.ts`:
  - `planRecordSuccess(currentRow: PostState, platformPostId: string): PostPatch`
    - Returns `{ status: 'published', platformPostId, publishedAt: <wall clock from caller>, bumpVersion: true }`
    - Throws `PostInvariantError({ kind: 'already_published' })` if the row already has a different `platformPostId`
  - `planRecordFailure(currentRow: PostState, failure: PublishFailure, isFinalAttempt: boolean): PostPatch`
    - If `failure.kind === 'permanent'` or `isFinalAttempt`: `{ status: 'failed', failureReason: <classified message>, bumpVersion: true }`
    - Otherwise (transient with retries remaining): `{ failureReason: <classified message>, lastAttemptAt, bumpVersion: false }` — status stays `publishing` so BullMQ's retry can resume
- Update `packages/worker/src/post-lifecycle.service.ts`:
  - Replace inline success-recording with `planRecordSuccess(...)`
  - Replace inline `recordFailureAttempt(...)` decision logic with `planRecordFailure(...)` — the worker still owns the `post_attempts` insert (audit row) and the notification-emit-after-commit ordering for `token_revoked`
- Unit tests for both: branch coverage on every decision path

## Out of scope

- Sweep / deletions (R2.5)
- Auto-destruct lifecycle (separate concern, separate aggregate if it ever exists)

## Acceptance

- `pnpm --filter @sms/shared build` and test pass
- `pnpm --filter @sms/worker build` and test pass
- Existing `recordFailureAttempt` tests pass without modification
- The "persist classification BEFORE the UnrecoverableError replaces the error object" pitfall (RESEARCH.md Pitfall 8 — top of `post-lifecycle.service.ts`) is preserved

## References

- ADRs: [0003](../blob/main/docs/adr/0003-post-lifecycle-is-pure-functions-not-shared-repository.md), [0004](../blob/main/docs/adr/0004-publish-cycle-orchestration-stays-in-the-worker.md)
- Depends on: #63
